angles: Testing React components powered by AI APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) requires special async/streaming patterns that standard RTL guides don't cover
Vercel AI SDK ships first-party test helpers (MockLanguageModelV3, simulateReadableStream) that allow fully deterministic tests without real LLM calls
MSW (Mock Service Worker) is now the gold standard for mocking AI API endpoints at the network layer — better than mocking fetch directly
AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) can dramatically accelerate test writing when given RTL-specific prompts and patterns
Testing streaming LLM responses requires specific patterns: simulateReadableStream + waitFor + findBy queries to handle progressive UI updates
Testing useChat and useCompletion hooks from Vercel AI SDK requires wrapping in providers and simulating SSE streams
The modern stack in 2026 is Vitest + RTL + MSW + user-event — NOT Jest for greenfield projects
Accessibility-first querying (getByRole, getByLabelText) is especially important for AI-generated UI content that may have unpredictable DOM output
